questions.question: Which of the following equations will be correct if the symbols ‘+’ and ‘×’ are interchanged and the numbers 40 and 10 are also interchanged?

In the following question, there is a certain relationship between two given letter pairs on one side of (::) and a pair is given on another side of (::) where another pair is to be found from the given alternatives, having the same relationship with this pair as the pairs of the given pair bear. Choose the best alternative. ED : DC :: GF : ?
questions.explanation: Pattern: Each letter is shifted one step backward in the alphabet. E → D, D → C Applying the same rule: G → F, F → E (Ans)

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed)? (3: 5: 7): (15: 25: 35):: (6: 10: 14) : (?)
Which of the following letter cluster will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? PZFS , RXHQ , TVJO , VTLM , ?
questions.explanation: Given series: PZFS, RXHQ, TVJO, VTLM, ? We analyze column-wise letter movement (position-wise pattern). Step-by-step analysis: Compare each position separately: 1st letters: P → R → T → V → ? Each step +2 letters forward So, 1st letter = X 2nd letters: Z → X → V → T → ? Each step −2 letters backward So, 2nd letter = R 3rd letters: F → H → J → L → ? Each step +2 letters forward So, 3rd letter = N 4th letters: S → Q → O → M → ? Each step −2 letters backward So, 4th letter = K Final Answer: XRNK ✅ Correct option: Option 1

questions.question: In this question given below is a statement followed by two causes. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the following suggested cause can be logically deduced from the statement. Statement: Over the last year, the sales of electric cars in the country have increased significantly, despite no major reduction in their prices. Cause: • The government has recently announced stricter emission norms for petrol and diesel vehicles. • Several major cities introduced dedicated charging infrastructure and reserved parking space for Electric vehicles.
options: Both I and II are cause.
Neither I nor II is a cause.
questions.explanation: → Stricter norms for petrol and diesel which can increase the price for conventional vehicles leading to sales of EVS Cause 1 Follows → Charging infrastructure and reserved parking spaced boosts EV adoption Cause 2 follows Opt 3

questions.question: Identify the incorrect number in the given number series: 2, 6, 15, 31, 55, 92
Three of the following letter clusters are alike in some manner and hence form a group. Select the one which is not part of the group?
questions.explanation: G S T H ↪ G opp → T S opp → H W K D P ↪ W opp → D K opp → P E R V F ↪ E opp → V R opp → I L V O F ↪ L opp → O V opp → E not F L V O F is odd Opt 2

questions.question: Three statements are given, followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All lawyers are politicians. No journalist is a politician. Some journalists are writers. Conclusions: 1. Some writers are not politicians. 2. No lawyer is a journalist.
questions.explanation: I → Follows II → follows Opt 4.

questions.question: If 2 is added to each even digit and 2 is subtracted from each odd digit of the number: 8396457 then what is the sum of the highest digit and the second lowest digit in the newly formed number?
questions.explanation: Given number: 8396457 Rule: Add 2 to each even digit and subtract 2 from each odd digit. Apply changes: 8 → 10 3 → 1 9 → 7 6 → 8 4 → 6 5 → 3 7 → 5 New digits: 10, 1, 7, 8, 6, 3, 5 Arrange in ascending order: 1, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10 Highest digit = 10 Second lowest digit = 3 Required sum = 10 + 3 = 13 Final Answer: 13

questions.question: Which of the following letter cluster and number will replace the double question mark (?) in the given series? EAR, 7, HDU, 16, KGX, 34, ?, ?
questions.explanation: Given series: EAR, 7, HDU, 16, KGX, 34, ?, ? The pattern alternates: Letter cluster → Number → Letter cluster → Number So we analyze letters separately and numbers separately. Step 1: Analyze the letter clusters Series: EAR → HDU → KGX → ? Check each position letter-wise: 1st letters: E → H → K → ? Each step +3 So next letter = N 2nd letters: A → D → G → ? Each step +3 So next letter = J 3rd letters: R → U → X → ? So next letter = A Thus next letter cluster: NJA Step 2: Analyze the number pattern Numbers: 7 → 16 → 34 → ? Pattern: 7 × 2 + 2 = 16 16 × 2 + 2 = 34 34 × 2 + 2 = 70 So next number = 70 Final Answer: NJA, 70

questions.question: In a certain coded language “PLATE” is coded as 270 and “BOWL” is coded as 208. What will be the code for “FRYPAN” in the same coded language?
questions.explanation: BOWL → 208 2+15+23+12=52×4=208 Pattern: - (Sum of face values) × (No. of letters) F R Y P A N : - 6 letters 6+18+25+16+1+14=80×6 ⇒ 480 Opt 1

questions.question: Six people: Arjun, Bhavya, Chetan, Divya, Ekta and Faiz attend a Physics workshop from Monday to Saturday but not necessarily in the same order. Each attends on a different day. Chetan attends immediately after the person who attends on Wednesday. Divya attends before Chetan but after Arjun. Faiz attends two days after Bhavya. Exactly two people attend between Arjun and Chetan. Ekta is the last person to attend. Divya attended the workshop on which day?
questions.explanation: Mon → Arjun Tue → Bhavya Wed → Divya Thu → Chetan Fri → Faiz Sat → Ekta Divya attended workshop on Wednesday. Opt 1.

questions.question: The given sentence below has a word in which the letters are jumbled up. Rearrange the letters of that word written in capital letters to form the correct word: “The classroom became NLTSEI when the teacher entered.”
questions.explanation: S I L E N T Opt 3

questions.question: In the given question, study the given address carefully and select the option which is most identical to the given address: Flat no-302, Shanti Residency 12, MG Road, Mumbai- 400069 • Flat no-302, Shanti Residency 12, MG Road, Mumbai- 400069 • Flat no-308, Shanti Residency 12, MG Road, Mumbai- 400069 • Flat no-302, Shanthi Residency 12, MG Road, Mumbai- 400069 • Flat no-302, Shanti Residency, 12 MG Road Mumbai- 400069
questions.explanation: Given address: Flat no-302, Shanti Residency 12, MG Road, Mumbai- 400069 We must choose the option that is exactly identical — meaning same spelling, same numbers, same punctuation, and same word order. Let’s check each option: Option A Flat no-302, Shanti Residency 12, MG Road, Mumbai- 400069 ✔ Completely identical to the given address ✅ Correct answer Option B Flat no-308 ❌ Number changed (302 → 308) Option C Shanthi Residency ❌ Spelling changed (Shanti → Shanthi) Option D Shanti Residency, 12 MG Road Mumbai ❌ Order and punctuation changed ✅ Correct Answer: Option A

questions.question: In a class of 40 students, Rohan ranks 12th from the top and Suresh ranks 18th from the bottom. If Rohan is 5 ranks ahead of Amit, what is the rank of Amit from the bottom?
questions.explanation: Rohan Rank from top → 12th Rohan is 5 ranks ahead of Amit Amit Rank → 12+5=17th From top Amit Rank from bottom :- 40-(17)+1 ⇒40-16 =24th Opt 4

questions.question: Germany is related to Bundestag in the same way as Russia is related to ___?
questions.explanation: Bundestag is the parliament of Germany. Duma is the parliament of Russia. Opt 4.

questions.question: Which of the following will come next in the series? B, D, H, P, ?
questions.explanation: पैटर्न: हर अक्षर की स्थिति पिछले अक्षर की स्थिति से दोगुनी है। D(4) = B(2) × 2 H(8) = D(4) × 2 P(16) = H(8) × 2 ⟹ F(6, 32) = P(16) × 2 इसलिए, F ही इसका उत्तर है।

questions.question: The following Venn diagram shows the city survey of startup employees with 3 skills: Python, Data Analyst and Digital Marketing. How many employees know exactly one skill?
questions.explanation: To find how many employees know exactly one skill, we add the values in the non-overlapping regions of each circle in the Venn diagram. From the diagram: • Only Python = 62 • Only Data Analyst = 45 • Only Digital Marketing = 78 Employees with exactly one skill: [62 + 45 + 78 = 185] ✅ Answer: 185 employees Quick rule for such questions: “Exactly one skill” = sum of the three outer (single-circle only) regions, excluding all intersections (two-skill and three-skill overlaps).

questions.question: Three of the following numbers are alike in some manner and hence form a group. Find the odd number? (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed)
Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term STREAM : TSERMA :: REPAIR : ?
questions.explanation: Opt 3

questions.question: In a school, it has been observed that the students of class IX and class X are increasingly distracted during lessons due to excessive mobile phone usage. Some teachers suggest a complete ban on mobile phones inside the campus. However, a few students argue that phones are sometimes required for educational apps and emergency contact. Which of the following could be the most appropriate course of action?
options: Impose a complete ban on mobile phones for all students.
Allow mobile phones but require students to deposit them at the school office during class hours.
Allow mobile phones without any restriction.
Ignore the issue as it is a personal matter of students.
questions.explanation: The school has to take a balanced decision Allowing mobile phones but requiring students to deposit them during school office hours ensures there is no distraction to studies and students will be able to contact their families during emergency as well. Opt 2.

questions.question: In a certain coded language ‘ECONOMICS’ is coded as: CNMCSIOOE. What will be the code for ‘GEOGRAPHY’ in the same coded language?
questions.explanation: Opt 1.

questions.question: A number arrangement machine when given an input of numbers , rearranges them following a particular rule. The following is an illustration of input and arrangement. Step 4 is the last step of the machine. Input: 42 15 63 24 36 51 Step 1: 15 42 63 24 36 51 Step 2: 15 24 42 63 36 51 Step 3: 15 24 36 42 63 51 Step 4: 15 24 36 42 51 63 As per rule followed in the above steps, determine the Step no. 2 for the input given below: Input: 54 18 72 27 45 36
questions.explanation: Pattern: - Arrange numbers in ascending order Depot : - 54 18 72 27 45 36 Step1 : - 18 54 72 27 45 36 Step2 : - 18 27 54 72 45 36 Opt 3

questions.question: Pointing to man in a photograph, Priyanka said: “He is the only son of my grandfather’s only daughter. My mother has no siblings and I have only one brother.” How is the man in the photograph related to Priyanka?
questions.explanation: Statement: Priyanka says: “He is the only son of my grandfather’s only daughter. My mother has no siblings and I have only one brother.” Step-by-step logic: • Grandfather’s only daughter = Priyanka’s mother (because it is clearly stated her mother has no siblings) • Only son of her mother = Priyanka’s brother • Priyanka also confirms: “I have only one brother.” So the man in the photograph must be that brother. ✅ Final Answer: Brother

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (4 , 6 , 48 ) (5, 7 , 70 ) (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed)?

Which of the following statements are correct? • Rajshekhar was a famous court poet of Pratihara King Mahendra Pal. • Karpoormanjari, Kavya Mimamsa and Bal Ramayana are some famous works by Rajshekhar. • Rajashekhar has described Mahendra Pal as 'Nirbhayraj' and 'Nirbhay Narendra' in his works
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct :- Rajashekhara was a renowned poet and scholar in the court of the Gurjara-Pratihara ruler Mahendrapala I (9th–10th century CE). Statement 2: Correct :- Rajashekhara wrote several important literary works, including: • Karpuramanjari • Kavyamimamsa • Bala Ramayana Statement 3: Correct:- In his works, Rajashekhara praised King Mahendrapala with titles such as “Nirbhayraj” and “Nirbhay Narendra,” highlighting the king’s bravery and authority.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the ‘Chief Minister Single Woman Self-Employment Scheme’ to make single women self-reliant?
questions.explanation: The Uttarakhand government launched the Chief Minister Single Women Self-Employment Scheme (Mukhyamantri Ekal Mahila Swarozgar Yojana) to economically empower single women such as widows, divorced, and abandoned women. Under this scheme, financial assistance of up to ₹2 lakh is provided to help them start self-employment activities and become self-reliant.

questions.question: What is the percentage share of population working in agricultural sector as per census of India 2011?
questions.explanation: According to the Census of India 2011, about 54.6% of India's workforce was engaged in the agricultural sector. This includes both cultivators and agricultural laborers. Agriculture was the largest source of employment in India at that time.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a host country for 23rd FIFA World Cup 2026?
questions.explanation: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be jointly hosted by three countries: the United States, Canada, and Mexico. Here are some important facts about the 2026 FIFA World Cup: • It will be the 23rd FIFA World Cup. • The tournament will be jointly hosted by three countries: • United States • Canada • Mexico This is the first World Cup hosted by three nations. • The tournament will expand from 32 teams to 48 teams for the first time. • A total of 104 matches will be played. • Teams will be divided into 12 groups of 4 teams each. The top two teams from each group plus the best 8 third-placed teams will advance to the Round of 32. • The opening match is planned to be held in Mexico City. • The final is expected to be played in New York/New Jersey at MetLife Stadium. • Mexico will become the first country to host the FIFA World Cup three times (1970, 1986, 2026). The United States previously hosted the tournament in 1994.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of the provisions with reference to the Seventh Constitutional amendment Act, 1956?
options: It provided for the establishment of High Courts for Union Territories.
It introduced the provisions for All-India Services including the Indian Forest Service.
It enabled the appointment of the same person as Governor for two or more states
It enabled one High Court serve for two or more states.
questions.explanation: The Seventh Constitutional Amendment Act, 1956 was mainly related to the reorganization of states and changes in the judicial and administrative structure. Key provisions included: • It allowed the establishment of High Courts for Union Territories. • It allowed the same person to be appointed as Governor for two or more states. • It allowed a single High Court to serve multiple states. However, the provision related to All-India Services was introduced by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976 (Article 312 expanded to include services like the Indian Forest Service). Therefore, option B is not related to the Seventh Amendment.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true regarding the RavanaPhadi cave at Aihole? 1.Nataraja is the important structure at this site. 2.This Nataraja image is surrounded on the right by four large saptamatrikas and on the left by three large ones. 3.The figures have slim, graceful bodies with long oval faces. 4.The temple shows a distinct feature of Pandya architecture of mixing and incorporation of several styles.
questions.explanation: • The Ravana Phadi Cave at Aihole, built during the Early Chalukya period (6th century CE), contains an important image of Nataraja (Shiva as the cosmic dancer). • The Nataraja sculpture is flanked by the Saptamatrikas (seven mother goddesses) with four figures on one side and three on the other. • The sculptures in this cave are known for their slim, graceful bodies and elongated oval faces, typical of early Chalukyan art. • The cave does not show Pandya architectural features; it belongs to the Chalukyan architectural tradition of Aihole. Therefore statement 4 is incorrect.

questions.question: Which artificial element has been provisionally named seaborgium by American researchers in honour of Nobel Laureate Glenn T Seaborg?
questions.explanation: Seaborgium is a synthetic (artificial) chemical element with atomic number 106. It was named in honour of Glenn T. Seaborg, an American nuclear chemist who contributed greatly to the discovery of several transuranium elements and won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1951.

questions.question: What is the name of the long-range glide bomb successfully tested by DRDO from a Su-30 MKI aircraft in 2025?
questions.explanation: The long-range glide bomb named Gaurav was successfully tested in 2025 by the Defence Research and Development Organisation from a Sukhoi Su-30MKI. It is an air-launched precision-guided weapon designed to strike targets at long distances.

questions.question: The term 'Ragam-Tanam-Pallavi' is associated with which style of Indian music?
questions.explanation: Ragam–Tanam–Pallavi (RTP) is a major improvisational form in Carnatic music. It is usually performed in concerts to showcase the musician’s creativity and mastery. • Ragam: Melodic improvisation of a raga without rhythm. • Tanam: Rhythmic improvisation of the raga. • Pallavi: A composed lyrical line used for complex rhythmic and melodic improvisation. This form is considered one of the most important and advanced elements of Carnatic classical music performances.

questions.question: What is the name of the fibrous connective tissue that connects bone to bone?
questions.explanation: Ligament is a connective tissue that attaches two bones with each other whereas Tendons are a type of connective tissue, which attach the muscles with bones.

questions.question: Who was nominated as the “Fit India Icon” by Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya during a three-day event in March 2025?
questions.explanation: During the first Fit India Carnival held in March 2025 at Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium, New Delhi, Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya nominated Ayushmann Khurrana as the “Fit India Icon” to promote fitness and a healthy lifestyle among citizens under the Fit India Movement.

questions.question: Which bee variety is commonly known as the Indian bee and is used for commercial honey production?
questions.explanation: Apis cerana indica is commonly called the Indian honey bee. It is widely used in traditional and commercial beekeeping in India because it adapts well to local climatic conditions and can be easily managed in hive boxes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about desert erosional landforms is/are correct? • Mushroom rocks are formed by water erosion and are typically found in glacial valleys. • Table rocks have a broad, flat top supported by a narrow base, resembling a plateau. • Pedestal rocks are remnants of resistant rocks shaped by wind deflation and abrasion.
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Incorrect - Mushroom rocks (also called pedestal rocks) are mainly formed by wind erosion in deserts, especially through abrasion near the ground. They are not formed by water erosion and are not typical of glacial valleys. Statement 2: Incorrect - Table rocks generally refer to flat-topped elevated landforms like mesas or plateaus formed by differential erosion, not by a narrow base. A narrow base with a wider top is characteristic of mushroom or pedestal rocks. Statement 3: Correct - Pedestal rocks are created by wind deflation and abrasion in desert environments, where the lower part of the rock erodes faster than the upper part. Therefore, only statement 3 is correct.

questions.question: Who is the first woman to lead Indian Army unit near China border in Ladakh?
questions.explanation: Colonel Geeta Rana became the first woman officer to command an Indian Army unit deployed near the China border in Ladakh. She was given command of an engineering unit in the strategically important forward area, marking a significant step toward greater inclusion of women in operational leadership roles in the Indian Army. Punita Arora was the first woman in India to reach the rank of Lieutenant General in the Indian Armed Forces. Shuchita Shekhar is known for becoming one of the first women officers to command an Army Service Corps unit in the Indian Army. Madhuri Kanitkar is a senior officer of the Armed Forces Medical Services and served as the Vice-Chancellor of Maharashtra University of Health Sciences.

questions.question: The Quit India Resolution was ratified in the _________ session of Indian National Congress to launch the movement.
questions.explanation: The Quit India Resolution was passed by the Indian National Congress at its Bombay session on 8 August 1942 at Gowalia Tank Maidan (now August Kranti Maidan). This resolution led to the launch of the Quit India Movement under the leadership of Mahatma Gandhi with the famous call “Do or Die

questions.question: Which of the following pair is correct regarding government budget? I. Revenue Receipts – Custom duties II. Capital Receipts – Income tax III. Capital Receipts – PSU disinvestment
questions.explanation: I. Revenue Receipts – Custom duties Custom duties are taxes on imports and are a part of revenue receipts because they do not create liabilities and do not reduce government assets. So statement I is correct. II. Capital Receipts – Income tax Income tax is a tax collected by the government and comes under revenue receipts, not capital receipts. Therefore statement II is incorrect. III. Capital Receipts – PSU disinvestment Money received from disinvestment of Public Sector Undertakings reduces government assets and is classified as a capital receipt. So statement III is correct. Therefore the correct answer is Only I and III.

questions.question: NALCO is an Indian government enterprise, dealing with which of the following minerals?
questions.explanation: National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO) is a Government of India PSU under the Ministry of Mines. It is primarily involved in the mining of bauxite, refining it into alumina, and producing aluminium. Bauxite is the main raw material used for aluminium production. NALCO was established in 1981. Its headquarters is in Bhubaneswar. It operates under the Ministry of Mines. NALCO mainly deals with Bauxite, which is used to produce alumina and aluminium. It is a Navratna public sector enterprise of the Government of India.

questions.question: Who has won the 2025 Ramon Magsaysay Award?
questions.explanation: The 2025 Ramon Magsaysay Award was awarded to Educate Girls, an Indian NGO founded by Safeena Husain. The organization was recognized for its work in promoting girls’ education and reducing gender inequality in rural India. Ramon Magsaysay Award :- The award was established in 1957. This is often called “Asia’s Nobel Prize.” The foundation is located in Manila, Philippines. The first Indian recipient was Vinoba Bhave in 1958.

questions.question: How many Lok Sabha seats are there for the state of Mizoram?
questions.explanation: Mizoram sends only one representative to the Lok Sabha. The state has a single parliamentary constituency called the Mizoram Lok Sabha constituency. State Lok Sabha Seats Assam 14 Arunachal Pradesh 2 Manipur 2 Meghalaya 2 Mizoram 1 Nagaland 1 Tripura 2 Sikkim 1

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Comprehensive Environmental Pollution Index?
options: It shall be an early warning tool
All the polluting sources identified in the area shall be notified and brought into the public domain
It can applied to locations other than industrial clusters
questions.explanation: The Comprehensive Environmental Pollution Index (CEPI) was developed by the Central Pollution Control Board in 2009 to assess pollution levels in industrial clusters across India. Key features of CEPI: It acts as an early warning tool to identify critically polluted areas. It evaluates pollution based on air, water, and land indicators. Polluting sources identified in an area are notified and placed in the public domain for transparency. It can also be applied to locations beyond industrial clusters if needed.

questions.question: When we hit a table lightly, the sound produced is soft because the:
questions.explanation: The loudness of a sound depends on the amplitude of vibration of the object producing the sound. When the table is hit lightly, it vibrates with a small amplitude, so the sound produced is soft. Frequency mainly affects the pitch of sound, not its loudness.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book 'Leo: The Untold Story of Chennai Super Kings', which was launched in March 2025?
questions.explanation: The book “Leo: The Untold Story of Chennai Super Kings” was written by P. S. Raman, a senior advocate and former vice-president of the Tamil Nadu Cricket Association. The book provides an insider account of the journey, challenges, and successes of the IPL franchise Chennai Super Kings and was launched in March 2025

questions.question: In which year was the first Cricket World Cup held in India?
questions.explanation: The first Cricket World Cup held in India took place in 1987. It was jointly hosted by India and Pakistan. Earlier editions of the World Cup (1975, 1979, 1983) were hosted in United Kingdom. The 1987 tournament was the first time the event was held outside England.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct regarding State Legislatures? I. The work and discussions in a State Legislature can be conducted in the official language of the state, or in Hindi, or in English. However, this must follow the rules given in Article 348 of the Constitution. II. The Speaker of the Legislative Assembly or Chairman of the Legislative Council can permit any member to speak in his mother tongue. III. Only the Supreme Court is entitled to inquire into the proceeding of the State Legislature.
questions.explanation: Statement I: Correct According to Article 210 of the Constitution of India, the business of a State Legislature may be conducted in the official language(s) of the state, or in Hindi or English, subject to Article 348. Statement II: Correct Under the same article, the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly or the Chairman of the Legislative Council may permit a member to address the House in his or her mother tongue. Statement III: Incorrect According to Article 212 of the Constitution of India, courts cannot inquire into the proceedings of the State Legislature on grounds of procedural irregularity. It is not specifically only the Supreme Court that can inquire; generally, the judiciary is restricted from interfering in legislative proceedings. Therefore, the correct answer is Only I and II.

questions.question: Dussehra is celebrated in ________ month of the Hindu calendar.
questions.explanation: Dussehra is celebrated on the tenth day (Dashami) of the bright half (Shukla Paksha) of the Hindu lunar month of Ashvina. It marks the victory of Rama over Ravana and symbolizes the victory of good over evil. • Dussehra → Ashvina • Diwali → Kartika • Holi → Phalguna • Janmashtami → Shravana • Ganesh Chaturthi → Bhadrapada

questions.question: In ABC, AB= BC and D is a point inside ∆ABC such that ∠DAC = ∠DCA. Consider the following statements: - I. ∆ABC is an isosceles II. D is the centroid of ∆ABC III. ABD is congruent to ∆CBD Which of the following is correct?
options: Only I and II are correct
Only I and III are correct
questions.explanation: AB=BC ∠DAC= ∠DCA ∴AD=CD (i) ABC isosceles (AB = BC) (iii) ABD & DBC:- BD common AB = BC (Given) AD = CD ∴ABD≅DBC Only (i) & (iii)

questions.question: Sum of LCM & HCF of two numbers is 857 and LCM is 235 more than HCF. The product of two numbers is?
The population of a town increased from 268000 in 2007 to 375200 in 2010. What is the percentage increase in the population of the town in three years?
If X+1/X = 6 then find the value of X3 + 1/X3.
If the gain is two-seventh of the selling price, then the gain percentage is:
questions.explanation: Gain=2/7 →gain ∴ CP = (7 – 2) = 5 Actual gain percentage = 2/5×100=40%

questions.question: A man can row a boat at 10km/h in still water. If the speed of the stream is 7km/h, what is the time taken (in hours) to row a distance of 85 km down the stream?
questions.explanation: Sol: x=10km/h, y=7km/h Speed down the stream = (x+y)=17km/hr Time taken = 85/17=5hr.

questions.question: The ratio of an amount invested and the amount received is 5:8 in 5 years at simple interest. Find rate of interest.
Find the value of the following:
If the numerical value of total surface area is equal to the volume of the cube, then find the diagonal of cube?
questions.explanation: Sol: TSA = Volume 6a2 = a3 ⇒ a = 6 Diagonal = a√3=6√3

questions.question: A shopkeeper earns a profit of 21% after selling a book at 21% discount on the printed price. The ratio of the cost price and selling price of the book is:
questions.explanation: Sol: Let CP = 100 Profit = 21% CP : SP = 100 : 121 ∴ CP = 121

questions.question: The ratio of measures of the angles of a triangle is given as 3:4:8. What is the measure of the smallest of the three angles?
questions.explanation: Sol: 3x+4x+8x=180° 15x=180°⇒x=12 Smallest angle = 3x = 36°

questions.question: A and B are two pipes which can fill a tank individually in 20 minutes and 25 minutes respectively, however there is a leakage at the bottom of tank which can empty the filled tank in 30 minutes. If the tank is empty initially, how much time will both the taps take to fill the tank (leakage is still there)?
What is the value of x for which x, x+1 and x+3 all are prime number?
questions.explanation: Sol: we can check by options. (0 is not a prime number) Option B follows.

questions.question: If ptanx+ qsecx= a and qtanx+ psecx= b. Then, find the value of a2−b2.
questions.explanation: a = p tan x + q sec x a² = p² tan² x + q² sec² x + 2pq tan x sec x b = q tan x + p sec x b² = q² tan² x + p² sec² x + 2pq tan x sec x a² - b² = tan² x (p² - q²) - sec² x (p² - q²) a² - b² = (p² - q²)(tan² x - sec² x) a² - b² = q² - p²

questions.question: In what ratio should rice at ₹65 per kg be mixed with rice at ₹40 per kg such that by selling the mixture at ₹63 per kg, there is a gain of 12.5%?
From a sampled data: Mean= 80, Median= 65. Find Mode?
If the amount on a certain principal in 3 years at 12% rate of interest compounded annually is Rs. 12,000 what will be the amount (in Rs.) after the 4th year?
questions.explanation: Sol: Amount after 4th year = 12000×112/100 = 13440

questions.question: From the top of a pole of height 50√3 feet high, the angle of elevation of the top of the upright tower was 450. If the feet of the pole is 25 feet away from the foot of the tower, then what is the height of the tower?
The distance travelled by a person varies directly with the duration of the journey. If the person travels 378 km in 7 hours, in how many hours will the person cover 918 km?
questions.explanation: Sol: Speed = 378/7=54km/h Time = 918/54=17 hours

questions.question: For what value of m will the system of equations: 22x−88y +17=0 and 15x – my – 13=0 have no solutions?
A and B can do a piece of work in 24 days. B and C together can do it in 36 days. If A is twice as good as a workman as C. B alone can do the work in?
questions.explanation: Sol: ⇒ (A+B)×24 = (B+C)×36 Given, A/C = 2/1 So, (A+B)/(B+C) = 3/2 ⇒ 2A = B + 3C ⇒ 2×2 = B + 3×1 ⇒ B = 1 unit/day total work = (A+B)×24 = (2+1)×24 = 72 unit B can do the whole work in = ((2+1)×24)/1 = (3×24)/1 = 72 days

questions.question: The volume and height of a Right circular cone are 2910.6 cm3 and 25.2 cm find its C.S.A?
The incomes of A, B and C are in the ratio of 8:9:6 and their spending in the ratio of 12:15:10. If A saves 25% of his Income. What is the ratio of the savings of C, A, B?
questions.explanation: Sol: Incomes of A, B and C- 8∶9∶6 Expense in the ratio – 12∶15∶10 ⇒ 75%A=12 A = 16 Income of A, B, C 16∶18∶12 Exp – 12∶18∶12 Savings – (4∶3∶2)/(A B C)

questions.question: Three cards are drawn at random from a pack of 52 cards. Find the probability of getting all three cards of same number?
questions.explanation: Same no. (1,1,1), (2,2,2) etc- Probability of getting 3 same numbers out of 4 same ones = 4C3 There are 13 different cards. Therefore, the favorable outcomes = 13×4C3 Total outcomes = 52C3 Hence, the probability that they all are same = = 13×4C3/52C3 = (13×4)(3×2)/(52×51×50) = 1/425

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: The Chairman, along with the Board of Directors and the primary
stakeholders have decided to postpone the annual general
meeting due to the unforeseen volatility in the global markets.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 👉 Replace "have" with "has". 📌 Error concept: The error lies in Subject-Verb Agreement. When a singular subject is connected to other nouns/pronouns using words like "along with," the verb must agree with the first subject only. Grammatical concept: • When two subjects are joined by phrases such as: along with, together with, as well as, in addition to, accompanied by etc. The verb always follows the first subject (Subject 1). • In this sentence, "The Chairman" is the primary subject (singular), while "the Board of Directors" and "primary stakeholders" are additional information. Therefore, the helping verb must be singular (has) to match "The Chairman."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Despite the initial media frenzy surrounding the corporate scandal, the CEO remained confident that the public's outrage would eventually __________ without requiring a massive restructuring of the board.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - The correct phrasal verb is "blow over," which means to pass or end without having serious consequences. In the context of the sentence, the CEO believes the public's anger (outrage) will eventually subside or be forgotten without forcing major changes. Meanings and Explanation of Options: • Blow over - (किसी विवाद या समस्या का) शांत हो जाना / टल जाना - To subside or pass away without leaving serious effects; to be forgotten. Example: I thought that the argument would blow over in a few days, and it did. • Blow out - बुझाना - To extinguish (a flame, etc.) by blowing. Example: She blew out the candles on her birthday cake. • Blow up - धमाका होना / बहुत गुस्सा होना - To explode; or to suddenly become very angry. Example: The rebels tried to blow up the bridge with dynamite. • Blow off - अनदेखा करना / टालना (लापरवाही से) - To ignore someone or something; to fail to attend or take seriously. Example: He decided to blow off the meeting to go to the gym instead.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The serene atmosphere of the countryside is often considered more conducive for mental well-being and productivity than the chaotic environment of a bustling metropolis.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - The word "conducive" means "making a certain situation or outcome likely or possible" (सहायक या अनुकूल). In English grammar, it is a rule of fixed prepositions that conducive is followed by "to", not "for". Structure: Something is + conducive + to + something. Example: Regular exercise is conducive to happiness.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: No sooner had the project been approved when the team began their intensive research phase.
The new software is including several features that will significantly enhance data security for all users.
Not having understood the complex instructions, the entire apparatus was assembled incorrectly by the technician.
Scarcely had the speaker finished his presentation when the audience erupted in thunderous applause.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct. It uses the rule of Inversion. When a sentence begins with a negative adverb or adverbial phrase like "Scarcely," "Hardly," or "Rarely," the auxiliary verb (had) must come before the subject (the speaker). Detailed Analysis of Other wrong Options: • Option A: Error: The conjunction "when" should be replaced with "than." When a sentence begins with "No sooner," it is always followed by the correlative conjunction "than." The conjunction "when" is used only with words like Hardly, Scarcely, or Barely. • Option B: Error: It should be "The new software includes..." The verb "include" is a stative verb (representing a state or characteristic). Stative verbs are generally not used in the continuous/progressive tense ("is including"). To state a fact or a permanent characteristic, the Simple Present Tense should be used. • Option C: Error: The sentence contains a "Dangling Participle." The phrase "Not having understood the complex instructions" implies that "the entire apparatus" (the subject of the main clause) did not understand the instructions, which is logically impossible. It should be: "Not having understood the complex instructions, the technician assembled the entire apparatus incorrectly."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. By the time the new recruitment notification is released, the serious aspirants will finish their entire syllabus twice.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 📌 Explanation - The sentence follows the structure of the Future Perfect combined with Present Indefinite. The action of finishing the syllabus will be completed before the notification is released in the future. Therefore, "will finish" must be improved to "will have finished." Grammatical concept: This is a specific conditional structure used to show that one action will be completed before another action takes place in the future. The Rule: • Clause 1: By the time + Subject + V1/V{s/es} (Present Indefinite) • Clause 2: Subject + will/shall + have + V3 (Future Perfect)

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The diplomat’s ________ remarks during the international summit managed to de-escalate the tension between the two neighboring nations without compromising national interests.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The correct word is "conciliatory," which refers to actions or remarks intended to pacify someone or reconcile a situation. Since the diplomat successfully "de-escalated tension," his remarks must have been peaceful and intended to soothe the conflict. Meanings and Explanation of Options: • Conciliatory - मैत्रीपूर्ण / समझौताकारी - Intended or likely to placate or pacify; tending to overcome distrust or hostility. Example: The President’s conciliatory gesture helped end the long-standing strike. • Abrasive - कठोर / रगड़ने वाला (व्यवहार में रूखा) - Showing little concern for the feelings of others; harsh or rough in manner. Example: Her abrasive personality made it difficult for her to keep friends for long. • Provocative - उकसाने वाला / उत्तेजक - Causing annoyance, anger, or another strong reaction, especially deliberately. Example: The politician's provocative speech led to widespread protests across the city. • Scathing - तीखा / बहुत कठोर (आलोचना) - Severely critical and scornful; extremely harsh. Example: The director received a scathing review of his latest movie in the newspapers.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: Although he had a little knowledge of quantum mechanics,
he managed to impress the interview panel with
his profound understanding of theoretical physics.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 👉 Replace "a little" with "little" 📌 Error concept: The error involves the incorrect use of the quantifier "a little" in a context that requires a negative sense. The word "Although" indicates a contradiction between having almost no knowledge and still being able to impress others. Grammatical concept: • Little: This quantifier carries a negative sense and denotes "hardly any" or "almost nothing." It is used when the quantity is negligible or insufficient for the purpose. • A little: This quantifier carries a positive sense and denotes "some amount," representing a small but existing quantity. • The little: This quantifier refers to "not much, but all that is available." It indicates the specific total of a small quantity mentioned in the context. In competitive exams, "Little" is used to highlight a lack or deficiency, whereas "A little" is used to acknowledge presence. When a sentence begins with a contrastive conjunction like "Although" or "Even though," the negative form "Little" is typically required to create the logical opposition needed for the sentence to remain semantically coherent.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The ________ jurist argued that a recession was ________ if the central bank did not intervene immediately to stabilize the plummeting currency.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - This question tests the knowledge of Homonyms (words that sound similar but have different meanings). • The first blank requires an adjective to describe the jurist (a person). "Eminent" is used for famous or respected people. • The second blank describes a recession that is about to happen very soon. "Imminent" is used for events that are fast approaching. • Eminent - प्रख्यात / प्रसिद्ध - (Of a person) famous and respected within a particular sphere or profession. Example: The eminent scientist was awarded the Nobel Prize for his contribution to physics. • Imminent - करीब / शीघ्र होने वाला - About to happen; fast approaching Example: The dark clouds suggested that a rainstorm was imminent.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P: In contrast, a decentralized fiscal policy allows local governments to tailor their spending according to the specific needs of their constituents. Q: Economists often debate the merits of a centralized financial system versus a more distributed approach to resource allocation. R: A centralized system can ensure uniformity and large-scale infrastructure development across a nation. S: Ultimately, the most effective model often involves a hybrid strategy that balances national priorities with local requirements.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) - QRPS. 📌 Explanation - • Opening Statement: Sentence Q introduces the main theme of the debate—centralized versus distributed (decentralized) systems. This is the logical starting point. • First Side of Argument: Sentence R follows Q because it explains the benefit of the first system mentioned (Centralized). It focuses on "uniformity" and "large-scale infrastructure." • Contrast: Sentence P starts with the connector "In contrast," which directly opposes the idea in R. It explains the benefit of the second system (Decentralized/Local). • Conclusion: Sentence S uses the word "Ultimately" to provide a concluding thought, suggesting a "hybrid strategy" that combines both methods. Final Paragraph: Economists often debate the merits of a centralized financial system versus a more distributed approach to resource allocation. A centralized system can ensure uniformity and large-scale infrastructure development across a nation. In contrast, a decentralized fiscal policy allows local governments to tailor their spending according to the specific needs of their constituents. Ultimately, the most effective model often involves a hybrid strategy that balances national priorities with local requirements.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The committee is meticulously scrutinizing the financial discrepancies found in the quarterly audit report.
options: The financial discrepancies found in the quarterly audit report were being meticulously scrutinized by the committee.
The financial discrepancies found in the quarterly audit report are being meticulously scrutinized by the committee.
The financial discrepancies found in the quarterly audit report are meticulously scrutinized by the committee.
The financial discrepancies found in the quarterly audit report has been meticulously scrutinized by the committee.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The original sentence is in the Present Continuous Tense (is + scrutinizing). To change it into passive voice, we must use the structure: [Object] + is/am/are + being + V3 + by + [Subject]. • Subject: The committee • Verb: is scrutinizing • Object: The financial discrepancies (Plural) Since the object "discrepancies" is plural, we use "are being scrutinized."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The manager said, "While the technicians were repairing the server, the power supply suddenly failed."
options: The manager said that while the technicians had been repairing the server, the power supply had suddenly failed.
The manager said that while the technicians were repairing the server, the power supply had suddenly failed.
The manager said that while the technicians were repairing the server, the power supply has suddenly failed.
The manager said that while the technicians were repairing the server, the power supply suddenly failed.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - This is a special case in Narration. Usually, Past Continuous changes to Past Perfect Continuous, and Simple Past changes to Past Perfect. However, when two actions happen simultaneously or are connected by "while/when" in the past, the tenses often remain unchanged to maintain the logical timeline of the background action. Grammatical concept: Rule for Time Clauses (While/When): When the Reported Speech contains a time clause (starting with when or while) describing two actions occurring at the same time in the past, we generally do not change the tense. • Logic: Changing the tense to "had been" or "had + V3" can sometimes distort the sense of two things happening together. • Exception: If the sentence describes a sequence where one action was completely finished before another, the tense change is more likely. But for simultaneous background actions (While...), the tenses are preserved.

questions.question: Bengaluru is currently grappling with a severe water crisis. While the state’s groundwater extraction remains within sustainable limits, Bengaluru East has reached a staggering 378% extraction rate. This situation is ____(1)_____ of an ongoing crisis that has turned acute. The city's geography, characterized by crystalline rock, already restricts natural recharge. Furthermore, the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure ____(2)_____ the ground’s ability to absorb rainwater. Despite government efforts to supply water to parched villages, the project has only achieved partial success, leaving residents ____(3)______ on private tankers. Experts suggest that the city must pivot toward becoming a ‘sponge city’ to ____(4)_____ monsoon runoff. Unless authorities mandate recycled water usage and penalize over-extraction, the ecological capital of the city will continue to ____(5)_____. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 💢 Explanation - The word "Reminiscent" means याद दिलाने वाला Tending to remind one of something or being suggestive of a previous condition. In this context, the extraction rate is suggestive or a reflection of an ongoing crisis. Fixed Preposition: The word Reminiscent is always followed by the preposition 'of'. Explanation for other options: • Repentant: Feeling or expressing remorse (पछतावा करने वाला). Wrong because a situation cannot feel remorse. • Resonant: Deep, clear, and continuing to sound; or suggesting images/memories (गूँजने वाला). Wrong because it doesn't fit the context of indicating a crisis. • Reluctant: Unwilling and hesitant (अनिच्छुक). Wrong as the rate isn't "unwilling" of a crisis.

questions.question: Bengaluru is currently grappling with a severe water crisis. While the state’s groundwater extraction remains within sustainable limits, Bengaluru East has reached a staggering 378% extraction rate. This situation is ____(1)_____ of an ongoing crisis that has turned acute. The city's geography, characterized by crystalline rock, already restricts natural recharge. Furthermore, the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure ____(2)_____ the ground’s ability to absorb rainwater. Despite government efforts to supply water to parched villages, the project has only achieved partial success, leaving residents ____(3)______ on private tankers. Experts suggest that the city must pivot toward becoming a ‘sponge city’ to ____(4)_____ monsoon runoff. Unless authorities mandate recycled water usage and penalize over-extraction, the ecological capital of the city will continue to ____(5)_____. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 💢 Explanation - To "Aggravate" means बदतर कर देना - To make a problem or situation worse. Urban expansion makes the lack of water absorption worse. Explanation for other options: • Facilitates: To make an action or process easy or easier (सुगम बनाना). Urban expansion does not make absorption easier; it blocks it. • Suppresses: Forcibly put an end to (दबाना/रोकना). While close, "aggravate" better describes making the overall problem worse. • Stimulates: To raise levels of physiological or nervous activity (उत्तेजित करना). Incorrect as infrastructure doesn't encourage absorption.

questions.question: Bengaluru is currently grappling with a severe water crisis. While the state’s groundwater extraction remains within sustainable limits, Bengaluru East has reached a staggering 378% extraction rate. This situation is ____(1)_____ of an ongoing crisis that has turned acute. The city's geography, characterized by crystalline rock, already restricts natural recharge. Furthermore, the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure ____(2)_____ the ground’s ability to absorb rainwater. Despite government efforts to supply water to parched villages, the project has only achieved partial success, leaving residents ____(3)______ on private tankers. Experts suggest that the city must pivot toward becoming a ‘sponge city’ to ____(4)_____ monsoon runoff. Unless authorities mandate recycled water usage and penalize over-extraction, the ecological capital of the city will continue to ____(5)_____. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 💢 Explanation - The phrasal verb "Banking on" means (निर्भर होना) To rely on or depend on something/someone. Since the project failed, residents are relying on tankers. Explanation for other options: • Banked up: To form into a pile or mass (ढेर लगाना). Example: The snow banked up against the door. • Banking with: To keep your money in a particular bank (बैंक में खाता होना). Example: I have been banking with HDFC for years. • Banked away: To store something, usually money, for future use (बचाकर रखना). Example: She banked away a portion of her salary.

questions.question: Bengaluru is currently grappling with a severe water crisis. While the state’s groundwater extraction remains within sustainable limits, Bengaluru East has reached a staggering 378% extraction rate. This situation is ____(1)_____ of an ongoing crisis that has turned acute. The city's geography, characterized by crystalline rock, already restricts natural recharge. Furthermore, the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure ____(2)_____ the ground’s ability to absorb rainwater. Despite government efforts to supply water to parched villages, the project has only achieved partial success, leaving residents ____(3)______ on private tankers. Experts suggest that the city must pivot toward becoming a ‘sponge city’ to ____(4)_____ monsoon runoff. Unless authorities mandate recycled water usage and penalize over-extraction, the ecological capital of the city will continue to ____(5)_____. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 💢 Explanation - "Capture" means पकड़ना या संचय करना - To record or take possession of something. A "sponge city" is designed to capture (collect/store) monsoon runoff instead of letting it waste away. Explanation for other options: • Rupture: To break or burst suddenly (फटना). Example: The pipe might rupture under pressure. • Enrapture: To give intense pleasure or joy to (मंत्रमुग्ध करना). Example: The music enraptured the audience. • Nurture: To care for and encourage the growth of (पोषण करना). You don't "nurture" runoff water; you collect it.

questions.question: Bengaluru is currently grappling with a severe water crisis. While the state’s groundwater extraction remains within sustainable limits, Bengaluru East has reached a staggering 378% extraction rate. This situation is ____(1)_____ of an ongoing crisis that has turned acute. The city's geography, characterized by crystalline rock, already restricts natural recharge. Furthermore, the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure ____(2)_____ the ground’s ability to absorb rainwater. Despite government efforts to supply water to parched villages, the project has only achieved partial success, leaving residents ____(3)______ on private tankers. Experts suggest that the city must pivot toward becoming a ‘sponge city’ to ____(4)_____ monsoon runoff. Unless authorities mandate recycled water usage and penalize over-extraction, the ecological capital of the city will continue to ____(5)_____. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 💢 Explanation - "Dwindle" means to diminish gradually in size, amount, or strength. The city's ecological capital (resources) will decrease due to over-extraction. Explanation for other options: • Flourish: To grow or develop in a healthy or vigorous way (फलना-फूलना). Opposite of what is happening. • Accumulate: To gather together or acquire an increasing number (जमा करना). The resources are being lost, not gathered. • Escalate: To become or cause to become more intense or serious (तेजी से बढ़ना). Usually used for prices or conflicts, not ecological capital.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Conscientious Let’s see the meaning of all words: Architect - वास्तुकार - a person who designs buildings Example: The architect drew plans for the new school. Unanimous - सर्वसम्मत - fully in agreement Example: The vote was unanimous in favor of the proposal. Innuendo - व्यंग्योक्ति - an indirect or subtle remark Example: Her comment was an innuendo about his past mistakes. Conscientious - कर्तव्यनिष्ठ - doing work carefully and thoroughly Example: He is conscientious and never misses a deadline.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The atmosphere in the room turned frosty after the argument.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Curt Let’s see the meaning of all words: Frosty - ठंडा - very cold or unfriendly Example: The atmosphere in the room turned frosty after the argument. Avarice - लालच - extreme greed for wealth Example: His avarice made him betray his own friends. Sleek - चिकना - smooth and glossy Example: She bought a sleek new car last week. Curt - रूखा - rudely brief or unfriendly Example: His curt reply ended the conversation quickly. Fowl - पक्षी - a bird, especially domestic Example: The farmer raises fowl for eggs and meat.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Delightful
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Unwelcome Let’s see the meaning of all words: Delightful - आनंददायक - giving great pleasure Example: The delightful music lifted everyone's mood. Unwelcome - अवांछित - not wanted or not pleasant Example: The unwelcome guest left early. Thick - मोटा - not thin Example: The book was too thick to read quickly. Delay - देरी - to make something happen later Example: The flight was delayed due to bad weather. Neat - साफ़ - tidy and organized Example: Her room was neat and clean.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Speaks volume
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Gives enough proof Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Speaks volume - बहुत कुछ कह देना - gives strong evidence or indicates something clearly Example: Her silence on the matter speaks volume about her feelings.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Docile
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Gentle Let’s see the meaning of all words: Docile - विनम्र - quiet and obedient Example: The docile child behaved well with the guests. Gentle - मृदु - kind or soft Example: She has a gentle nature. Cheat - धोखा देना - to deceive Example: He cheated in the exam. Awful - भयानक - very bad Example: The movie was awful. Drag - खींचना - to pull something Example: He dragged the box across the room.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Nobody was surprised by his actions since he is known for his caginess.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Frankness Let’s see the meaning of all words: Caginess - चतुराई - being careful not to reveal information Example: His caginess about the surprise party was impressive. Loyalty - वफादारी - devotion to a person or cause Example: His loyalty to his friends is admirable. Fellowship - साथीपन - a group of people sharing interests Example: He received a fellowship to study abroad. Ruthless - निर्दयी - cruel or uncompromising Example: The ruthless businessman didn't care about feelings. Frankness - स्पष्टवादिता - being open and honest Example: I appreciate her frankness in telling me the truth.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. An event, a situation, or a period of time in somebody’s life, a novel, etc that is important or interesting in some way
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Episode Let’s see the meaning of all words: Urology - उरोलोजी - medical study of urinary system Example: He specializes in urology. Episode - प्रकरण - an event or situation in a story or life Example: This episode of her life was quite dramatic. Pang - तीव्र भावना - a sudden sharp feeling Example: She felt a pang of guilt. Festal - उत्सव से संबंधित - relating to a festival Example: The festal decorations looked beautiful.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. During the heated argument, he lost his head and said things he later regretted deeply.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Panic Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Lose his head - अपना आपा खोना - To become very angry or confused, often acting impulsively. Example: During the heated argument, he lost his head and said things he later regretted deeply.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Someone skilled in penmanship
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Calligrapher Let’s see the meaning of all words: Calligrapher - कलाकार जो सुंदर लिखता है - a person skilled in beautiful handwriting Example: The calligrapher crafted the invitation with elegance. Galling - परेशान करने वाला - annoying or frustrating Example: The galling delay was frustrating everyone. Creed - विश्वास - a set of beliefs or principles Example: Their creed emphasizes kindness and honesty. Dirge - शोक गीत - a sad song or hymn Example: The dirge played at the funeral was moving.
